flows.name: browser-test
flows.description: Open the Google login page, wait for the user to log in, then navigate to Gmail.

steps.name: Open Google login
steps.content:
# OPEN GOOGLE LOGIN PAGE

## PURPOSE

Open the Google account login page so the user can sign in.

## TOOLS

Use ONLY the browser_* tools.

## WORKFLOW

1. Use `browser_navigate` to go to: https://accounts.google.com
2. Use `browser_snapshot` to see the page structure
3. Take a `browser_screenshot` and save as `{{run.dir}}/login-page.png`
4. Write to `{{run.dir}}/_result.md`:
   - The page URL
   - Confirm the Google login page is displayed
   - Note that the user needs to log in manually in the browser window

gates.message: Screenshot of the login page must exist.

steps.name: Wait for login
steps.content:
# WAIT FOR USER LOGIN

## PURPOSE

Pause and wait for the user to complete the Google login in the browser window.

## WORKFLOW

1. Write to `{{run.dir}}/_result.md`:
   - Tell the user: "The Google login page is open in the browser window. Please log in to your Google account there. Once you're logged in, type 'done' to continue to Gmail."

steps.name: Navigate to Gmail
steps.content:
# NAVIGATE TO GMAIL

## PURPOSE

After the user has logged in, navigate to Gmail and confirm it loaded.

## TOOLS

Use ONLY the browser_* tools.

## WORKFLOW

1. Use `browser_navigate` to go to: https://mail.google.com
2. Wait a moment for the page to load fully
3. Use `browser_snapshot` to verify Gmail is showing the inbox
4. Take a `browser_screenshot` and save as `{{run.dir}}/gmail.png`
5. Write to `{{run.dir}}/_result.md`:
   - The page URL
   - Confirm Gmail loaded successfully
   - Note the account name or email visible on the page if any
   - List any visible inbox items or UI elements
